title: Parents' coming to America; grandparents' homestead and shingle camp near Troy
description: Parents' coming to America and Genesee. Grandparent's homestead and shingle camp near Troy. By wagon from Genesee to Troy.

title: Growing up on Cow Creek; ten children in a small house; move to Genesee
description: Growing up on Cow Creek. Ten children in a small house - most families were large. Family's move from farm to Genesee; sons moved out of the area.

title: Girls expected to stay close to home; rarely did outdoor work
description: Girls were expected to stay close to home. She rarely did outdoors work. Even when visiting, women were never idle with their hands.

title: Cooking for uncle's threshing crew; threshing crew; moving the cook wagon; harvest season
description: Cooking for uncle's threshing crew - responsibilities and hard work. The threshing crew- migrants. Moving the cook wagon. Course of the harvest season.

title: Father's dislike of town boys; their farming
description: Her father's dislike of town boys. Knowing husband for many years; their farming.

title: Two churches in early days of Cow Creek divided the community
description: Two churches in the early days of Cow Creek divided the community.

title: Union of the churches; confirmation; church activities; Ladies Aid raising money
description: More about union of the churches. Confirmation and other church activities for young people. Ladies Aid raised money for the church.

title: Circus parades; dancing despite church's disapproval; school; seasonal weather
description: Children mistook circus parades for the circus itself. Dancing despite church's disapproval. School. Weather more seasonal in early days.

title: The Rosensteins; Native American intermarriage
description: The Rosensteins, a Jewish family. Indian intermarriage.

title: Shivarees; father planted by signs; grandmother's stories
description: Shivarees. Father planted by signs. Grandmother told stories about the "little folks."

title: 1893 wet harvest; father borrowing money
description: 1893 wet harvest. Father borrowed money from Jake Kambitsch.

title: Getting by in the early days
description: Getting by with a great deal of self-sufficiency in the early days.

title: Norwegian newspaper from South Dakota; men talked politics; importance of music; children's play
description: Family got a Norwegian newspaper from South Dakota. Men talked politics, women didn't. Importance of music in her family. Children's play.
